location_id.location_id: A unique identifier for each physical location, serving as the primary key for tracking and referencing specific address entities.

address_1.address_1: The primary street address of the location, representing the main street name and building number for this address entity.

address_2.address_2: Secondary address information such as apartment, suite, or unit number, providing additional specificity to the location's address.

city.city: The city or municipality where the location is situated, indicating the urban area of this address entity.

state.state: The state, province, or region where the location is located, representing the administrative division of this address.

zip.zip: The postal or ZIP code for the location, used for mail delivery and geographic routing for this address entity.

county.county: The county or district where the location is situated, providing an additional administrative subdivision for this address.

location_source_value.location_source_value: The original identifier or code from the source system that provided this location data, representing the external reference for this address entity.

cohort_definition_id.cohort_definition_id: An identifier for the cohort definition or criteria that defines this cohort group, linking to the specific cohort type this subject belongs to.

subject_id.subject_id: A unique identifier for an individual person or subject, representing the main entity who is a member of one or more cohorts.

cohort_start_date.cohort_start_date: The date when this subject first met the criteria and entered this specific cohort, marking the beginning of their cohort membership period.

cohort_end_date.cohort_end_date: The date when this subject no longer met the criteria or exited this specific cohort, marking the end of their cohort membership period.

date.date: The date when the immunization was administered to the patient, representing the timing of this vaccination event.

patient.patient: An identifier for the individual who received the immunization, linking to the patient entity in the healthcare system.

encounter.encounter: An identifier for the healthcare visit or appointment during which the immunization was administered.

code.code: A unique code identifying the specific type of vaccine or immunization administered, serving as the primary identifier for the immunization type.

description.description: The name or detailed description of the vaccine or immunization given, providing human-readable context for the immunization code.

cost.cost: The monetary expense associated with administering this immunization, representing the financial aspect of the vaccination service.

care_site_id.care_site_id: A unique identifier for each healthcare facility or service location, serving as the primary key for the care site entity.

care_site_name.care_site_name: The name or designation of the healthcare facility, representing how this care site is commonly identified.

place_of_service_concept_id.place_of_service_concept_id: A reference to standardized healthcare location types (like hospital or clinic), classifying the nature of services provided at this care site.

location_id.location_id: A reference to geographical and address information, linking this care site to its physical location details.

care_site_source_value.care_site_source_value: The original identifier from the source data system, preserving the raw reference to this healthcare facility.

place_of_service_source_value.place_of_service_source_value: The original classification code from the source system, maintaining the raw service location type designation.

id.id: A unique identifier for each healthcare encounter, serving as the primary key for tracking individual patient visits or medical interactions.

start.start: The date and time when the encounter began, indicating the initiation timestamp of this medical visit.

stop.stop: The date and time when the encounter ended, showing the completion timestamp of this medical visit.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the patient who received care during this encounter, representing the individual receiving medical services.

provider.provider: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who delivered care, representing the medical professional or facility conducting the encounter.

encounterclass.encounterclass: The classification category of the encounter type, such as inpatient, outpatient, emergency, or other clinical setting classifications.

code.code: A medical coding identifier for the encounter type, typically from standardized coding systems like CPT or ICD, specifying the formal classification of the visit.

description.description: A textual description of the encounter type, providing human-readable details about the nature and purpose of the medical visit.

cost.cost: The financial cost associated with this encounter, representing the monetary value or charge for the medical services provided.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A medical coding identifier for the reason or purpose of the encounter, indicating why the patient sought medical care.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual description of the reason for the encounter, explaining in plain language why the medical visit occurred.

id.id: A unique identifier for each imaging study record, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual medical imaging procedures.

date.date: The date when the imaging study was performed, indicating the temporal context of this medical imaging procedure.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the patient who underwent the imaging study, representing the individual who received this medical imaging.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the specific medical encounter or visit when the imaging was ordered, connecting the study to a particular healthcare interaction.

body site code.body site code: A coded value identifying the anatomical location or region of the body being imaged in this study, classifying the target area for the procedure.

body site description.body site description: A textual description of the anatomical location being imaged, providing human-readable context for the body site code in this study.

modality code.modality code: A coded value representing the imaging technique or technology used in this study (e.g., X-ray, MRI, CT), classifying the type of imaging performed.

modality description.modality description: A textual description of the imaging modality used, providing human-readable context for the modality code in this study.

sop code.sop code: A coded value for the specific imaging procedure or protocol (Standard Operating Procedure) followed in this study, classifying the exact imaging method.

sop description.sop description: A textual description of the imaging procedure or protocol performed, providing human-readable context for the SOP code in this study.

start.start: The date and time when the allergy was first recorded or became active for the patient, indicating when this medical condition began.

stop.stop: The date and time when the allergy was resolved or ended for the patient, showing the duration of this medical condition if it's no longer active.

patient.patient: A unique identifier for the individual who has this allergy, representing the person affected by this medical condition.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the specific medical visit or encounter when this allergy was documented or addressed.

code.code: A standardized medical code identifying the specific allergen or allergic reaction type, classifying the nature of this allergy.

description.description: A textual explanation of the allergy including the allergen substance and reaction details, providing human-readable context for the coded allergy.

start.start: The date and time when the medical condition began or was first diagnosed for the patient, representing the onset of this health issue.

stop.stop: The date and time when the medical condition ended or was resolved, indicating the duration of this health issue if it's not ongoing.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the individual patient who has this medical condition, representing the person affected by this health issue.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the specific medical visit or encounter when this condition was documented, treated, or addressed.

code.code: A standardized code identifying the type of medical condition (e.g., ICD-10 code), serving as the primary classification for this health issue.

description.description: The human-readable name or description of the medical condition corresponding to the code, providing clinical context for this health issue.

person_id.person_id: Unique identifier for the individual who died, serving as the primary entity for tracking death events per person.

death_date.death_date: The calendar date when the death occurred, providing the temporal context for when the person died.

death_datetime.death_datetime: The precise date and time of death, offering more detailed temporal information about when the death event happened.

death_type_concept_id.death_type_concept_id: A categorical identifier classifying the type or circumstances of death, such as natural causes, accident, or other classifications.

cause_concept_id.cause_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier representing the medical cause of death, linking to a controlled vocabulary for consistent classification.

cause_source_value.cause_source_value: The original, unstandardized value or code for the cause of death as recorded in the source system before mapping to standardized concepts.

cause_source_concept_id.cause_source_concept_id: An identifier linking to the source concept system from which the cause of death information originated, indicating the coding system used.

cdm_source_name.cdm_source_name: The name of the Common Data Model (CDM) source system, serving as the primary identifier for this metadata record about a healthcare data source.

cdm_source_abbreviation.cdm_source_abbreviation: An abbreviated or short form of the CDM source system name, providing a concise reference to the healthcare data source.

cdm_holder.cdm_holder: The organization, institution, or entity that holds, maintains, or is responsible for this CDM source system.

source_description.source_description: A detailed description of the source system, explaining its purpose, scope, and characteristics as a healthcare data source.

source_documentation_reference.source_documentation_reference: A reference or link to documentation about the original source system, providing additional context and specifications.

cdm_etl_reference.cdm_etl_reference: A reference to documentation about the Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) process used to convert source data to the CDM format.

source_release_date.source_release_date: The date when the original source data was released or made available, indicating the timing of the underlying healthcare data.

cdm_release_date.cdm_release_date: The date when this specific version of the CDM-formatted data was released, showing when the transformed data became available.

cdm_version.cdm_version: The version of the Common Data Model specification used for this source system, indicating the data model standard followed.

vocabulary_version.vocabulary_version: The version of the standardized medical vocabulary or terminology used in this CDM source system for coding concepts.

cohort_definition_id.cohort_definition_id: A unique numeric identifier for each cohort definition, serving as the primary key for this table.

cohort_definition_name.cohort_definition_name: The human-readable name given to this specific cohort definition for identification purposes.

cohort_definition_description.cohort_definition_description: A detailed textual explanation of the purpose, criteria, or intent behind this cohort definition.

definition_type_concept_id.definition_type_concept_id: A foreign key linking to a concept that categorizes the methodology or type of this cohort definition (e.g., 'Cohort inclusion criteria').

cohort_definition_syntax.cohort_definition_syntax: The formal logic or computational code (e.g., SQL, OMOP CDM specification) that operationally defines the cohort's membership rules.

subject_concept_id.subject_concept_id: A foreign key linking to a concept that identifies the type of entity (e.g., 'Person', 'Provider') that is the subject of this cohort.

cohort_initiation_date.cohort_initiation_date: The date when this cohort definition was formally created or activated, providing a temporal reference for its lifecycle.

domain_concept_id_1.domain_concept_id_1: Identifies the domain or type of the first fact/entity in the relationship (e.g., person, condition, procedure), categorizing what kind of entity fact_id_1 represents.

fact_id_1.fact_id_1: The unique identifier for the first fact/entity participating in the relationship, serving as the primary reference point for this relationship record.

domain_concept_id_2.domain_concept_id_2: Specifies the domain or type of the second fact/entity in the relationship, categorizing what kind of entity fact_id_2 represents.

fact_id_2.fact_id_2: The unique identifier for the second fact/entity participating in the relationship, linked to fact_id_1 through the defined relationship.

relationship_concept_id.relationship_concept_id: Defines the type of relationship between the two facts/entities (e.g., 'parent of', 'caused by', 'associated with'), providing the semantic meaning of how they are connected.

id.id: A unique identifier for each care plan record, serving as the primary key for tracking individual medical care plans.

start.start: The date and time when the care plan was initiated, indicating when this medical plan began.

stop.stop: The date and time when the care plan was completed or discontinued, showing when this medical plan ended.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the patient who is the subject of this care plan, representing the individual receiving medical care.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the medical encounter during which this care plan was created, representing the clinical visit that generated this plan.

code.code: A medical classification code identifying the type or category of this care plan, providing standardized categorization of the medical intervention.

description.description: A textual description explaining the nature and details of this care plan, providing human-readable context about the medical approach.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A medical code identifying the clinical reason or diagnosis that prompted this care plan, categorizing why this medical intervention was needed.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual description explaining the clinical rationale for creating this care plan, providing context about the medical necessity.

device_exposure_id.device_exposure_id: A unique identifier for each device exposure event, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual medical device usage records.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient who was exposed to the medical device, representing the individual receiving the device treatment or monitoring.

device_concept_id.device_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the medical device used, classifying the type of device involved in this exposure event.

device_exposure_start_date.device_exposure_start_date: The calendar date when the device exposure began, indicating the starting day of medical device usage or monitoring.

device_exposure_start_datetime.device_exposure_start_datetime: The precise date and time when the device exposure began, providing exact temporal context for the start of device usage.

device_exposure_end_date.device_exposure_end_date: The calendar date when the device exposure ended, marking the conclusion day of medical device usage or monitoring.

device_exposure_end_datetime.device_exposure_end_datetime: The precise date and time when the device exposure ended, providing exact temporal context for the conclusion of device usage.

device_type_concept_id.device_type_concept_id: A concept identifier classifying the type or context of the device exposure event, such as inpatient, outpatient, or emergency use.

unique_device_id.unique_device_id: A unique identifier for the specific physical device instance used, allowing tracking of individual device units across multiple exposures.

quantity.quantity: The number of device units or the quantity involved in this exposure event, measuring the volume or count of devices used.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who administered or oversaw the device exposure, representing the responsible medical professional.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare visit during which the device exposure occurred, connecting the device usage to a specific patient encounter.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier providing more granular detail about the specific part of the visit when device exposure occurred, offering sub-visit context.

device_source_value.device_source_value: The original source code or identifier for the device as recorded in the source system, preserving the raw data representation.

device_source_concept_id.device_source_concept_id: A concept identifier from the source vocabulary system for the device, linking to the original coding scheme used for device classification.

condition_occurrence_id.condition_occurrence_id: A unique identifier for each instance of a medical condition occurrence, serving as the primary key for tracking individual condition events in the healthcare system.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient who experienced this condition, representing the individual associated with this medical event.

condition_concept_id.condition_concept_id: A standardized medical concept identifier for the specific condition diagnosed, linking to a controlled vocabulary system for consistent classification.

condition_start_date.condition_start_date: The date when this condition began or was first diagnosed, indicating the onset timing of the medical event.

condition_start_datetime.condition_start_datetime: The precise date and time when this condition began or was first diagnosed, providing detailed temporal context for the condition onset.

condition_end_date.condition_end_date: The date when this condition ended or resolved, if applicable, indicating the conclusion timing of the medical event.

condition_end_datetime.condition_end_datetime: The precise date and time when this condition ended or resolved, providing detailed temporal context for the condition conclusion.

condition_type_concept_id.condition_type_concept_id: A concept identifier describing the type or context of the condition diagnosis, such as inpatient, outpatient, or self-reported.

condition_status_concept_id.condition_status_concept_id: A concept identifier describing the current status of the condition, such as active, resolved, or chronic.

stop_reason.stop_reason: The reason why recording of this condition was stopped, explaining why documentation of this medical event ceased.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who documented or diagnosed this condition, representing the medical professional responsible for this record.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare visit during which this condition was documented, connecting the condition to a specific medical encounter.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier linking to detailed visit information when this condition was documented, providing more granular context about the healthcare encounter.

condition_source_value.condition_source_value: The original source code or value for this condition as it appeared in the source data, before standardization to controlled vocabulary.

condition_source_concept_id.condition_source_concept_id: A concept identifier from the source vocabulary system, linking to the original coding system used for this condition.

condition_status_source_value.condition_status_source_value: The original source code or value for the condition status as it appeared in the source data, before standardization to controlled vocabulary.

observation_period_id.observation_period_id: A unique identifier for each observation period record, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual observation time spans.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the person being observed during this period, representing the individual subject of the observation time frame.

observation_period_start_date.observation_period_start_date: The date when the observation period began, marking the commencement of the observation time span for tracking purposes.

observation_period_end_date.observation_period_end_date: The date when the observation period concluded, indicating the termination point of the observation time span.

period_type_concept_id.period_type_concept_id: A categorical identifier that classifies the type of observation period, providing context about the nature or purpose of the observation time frame.

start.start: The date and time when the medication administration began, indicating when this specific medication treatment was initiated for the patient.

stop.stop: The date and time when the medication administration ended, showing the duration or completion point of this medication treatment for the patient.

patient.patient: A unique identifier linking to the patient who received the medication, representing the individual for whom this medication was prescribed or administered.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the healthcare encounter (visit, admission, or appointment) during which this medication was administered or prescribed.

code.code: A standardized code (such as NDC or RxNorm) that uniquely identifies the specific medication, serving as the primary identifier for the drug substance.

description.description: The name and descriptive details of the medication, providing human-readable information about what drug was administered.

cost.cost: The monetary cost associated with this medication administration, representing the financial value of the drug for this specific instance.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A coded value indicating the medical reason or diagnosis for prescribing this medication, linking to the clinical justification for the treatment.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual description of the medical reason or condition that warranted this medication, explaining why this drug was prescribed or administered.

drug_exposure_id.drug_exposure_id: A unique identifier for each drug administration event, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual medication exposure records.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient who received the medication, representing the individual subject of this drug exposure event.

drug_concept_id.drug_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the specific medication administered, classifying the drug substance involved in this exposure.

drug_exposure_start_date.drug_exposure_start_date: The calendar date when the drug administration began, indicating the initiation date of this medication exposure.

drug_exposure_start_datetime.drug_exposure_start_datetime: The precise date and time when drug administration started, providing exact timing for the beginning of this exposure event.

drug_exposure_end_date.drug_exposure_end_date: The calendar date when the drug administration ended, marking the conclusion date of this medication exposure period.

drug_exposure_end_datetime.drug_exposure_end_datetime: The precise date and time when drug administration ended, providing exact timing for the termination of this exposure event.

verbatim_end_date.verbatim_end_date: The original end date as recorded in the source system before standardization, preserving the raw data for this exposure's conclusion.

drug_type_concept_id.drug_type_concept_id: A concept identifier classifying the type of drug exposure (e.g., prescription, inpatient medication, over-the-counter), categorizing the nature of this administration event.

stop_reason.stop_reason: The documented reason why the medication was discontinued, explaining the termination circumstance for this drug exposure.

refills.refills: The number of prescription refills authorized for this medication, measuring the replenishment allowance associated with this exposure.

quantity.quantity: The amount of drug dispensed or administered, quantifying the volume or count of medication involved in this exposure.

days_supply.days_supply: The number of days the medication supply is intended to last, measuring the expected duration covered by this drug exposure.

sig.sig: The prescription instructions (signatura) for how to take the medication, detailing the administration directions for this drug exposure.

route_concept_id.route_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the administration route (oral, intravenous, topical, etc.), specifying how the drug was delivered in this exposure.

lot_number.lot_number: The manufacturer's batch or lot number for the drug product, identifying the specific production batch involved in this exposure.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who prescribed or administered the medication, representing the clinician responsible for this drug exposure.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the clinical visit or encounter during which the drug was administered, connecting this exposure to a healthcare event.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier for more granular visit information when applicable, providing additional context about the specific visit details for this exposure.

drug_source_value.drug_source_value: The original code or identifier for the drug from the source system, preserving the raw medication information before standardization.

drug_source_concept_id.drug_source_concept_id: A concept identifier from the source vocabulary system, representing the drug concept as originally coded in the source data.

route_source_value.route_source_value: The original code or description for the administration route from the source system, preserving raw route information before standardization.

dose_unit_source_value.dose_unit_source_value: The original unit of measurement for drug dosage from the source system, preserving the raw dose unit information before standardization.

payer_plan_period_id.payer_plan_period_id: A unique identifier for each payer plan period record, serving as the primary key that tracks specific insurance coverage timeframes for individuals.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the individual who has this payer plan coverage, representing the person associated with this insurance period.

payer_plan_period_start_date.payer_plan_period_start_date: The date when this specific payer plan period began, indicating the starting point of insurance coverage under this arrangement.

payer_plan_period_end_date.payer_plan_period_end_date: The date when this specific payer plan period ended, marking the conclusion of insurance coverage under this arrangement.

payer_source_value.payer_source_value: The identifier or code for the payer/insurer providing coverage during this period, specifying which organization funded the healthcare services.

plan_source_value.plan_source_value: The identifier or code for the specific insurance plan type during this period, detailing the coverage structure and benefits available.

family_source_value.family_source_value: The identifier or code for the family or group associated with this payer plan, indicating membership in a particular insurance group or family unit.

id.id: A unique identifier for each organization record, serving as the primary key for tracking individual organizations in the system.

name.name: The official name of the organization, representing the entity's identity and branding.

address.address: The street address where the organization is physically located, providing the primary location information.

city.city: The city in which the organization operates, indicating the municipal jurisdiction of the organization's location.

state.state: The state or province where the organization is based, specifying the regional jurisdiction of the organization.

zip.zip: The postal or zip code for the organization's location, used for mail delivery and geographic classification.

phone.phone: The primary contact telephone number for the organization, enabling communication with the entity.

utilization.utilization: A metric measuring the organization's usage rate or capacity utilization, indicating operational efficiency or resource usage.

date.date: The date when the clinical observation or measurement was recorded, providing temporal context for when this data point was collected.

patient.patient: An identifier for the individual patient associated with this observation, linking the measurement to a specific person in the healthcare system.

encounter.encounter: An identifier for the specific medical visit or encounter during which this observation was made, providing context about the clinical setting.

code.code: A standardized identifier representing the type of clinical observation or measurement being recorded, such as a LOINC or SNOMED code for specific tests or assessments.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what the observation code represents, providing context about what is being measured or observed.

value.value: The actual numerical or categorical result of the observation or measurement, representing the clinical finding or test result.

units.units: The measurement units associated with the observation value, specifying how the numerical result should be interpreted (e.g., mg/dL, cm, etc.).

type.type: A classification category for the observation, indicating the general class or category of measurement (e.g., vital sign, lab test, assessment, etc.).

id.id: A unique identifier for each healthcare provider, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual provider records.

organization.organization: The organization, company, or healthcare facility where the provider works or is affiliated, representing their professional association.

name.name: The full name of the healthcare provider, identifying the individual person associated with this provider record.

gender.gender: The gender of the provider, categorizing them as male, female, or other for demographic and reporting purposes.

speciality.speciality: The professional specialization or medical field of the provider, indicating their area of expertise and practice focus.

address.address: The street address where the provider practices or is located, providing physical location information for this provider.

city.city: The city component of the provider's practice location, specifying the municipal area where they provide services.

state.state: The state or province where the provider is located, indicating the regional jurisdiction of their practice.

zip.zip: The postal or ZIP code for the provider's location, used for geographic sorting and regional analysis of provider distribution.

utilization.utilization: A metric measuring the provider's usage, activity level, or patient volume, quantifying their operational engagement or service delivery.

id.id: A unique identifier for each patient record, serving as the primary key for tracking individual patients in the healthcare system.

birthdate.birthdate: The date when the patient was born, representing the starting point of the patient's medical history timeline.

deathdate.deathdate: The date when the patient passed away, if applicable, marking the endpoint of the patient's medical record.

ssn.ssn: The patient's Social Security Number, a government-issued identifier used for official records and identification purposes.

drivers.drivers: The patient's driver's license number, providing an additional form of government-issued identification for the individual.

passport.passport: The patient's passport number, representing international travel documentation and identification for the individual.

prefix.prefix: The patient's name prefix (such as Mr., Mrs., Dr.), indicating honorific or title associated with the individual.

first.first: The patient's first or given name, representing the personal identifier used in daily interactions and records.

last.last: The patient's last name or surname, indicating family lineage and formal identification for the individual.

suffix.suffix: The patient's name suffix (such as Jr., Sr., III), denoting generational or professional designations for the individual.

maiden.maiden: The patient's maiden name, typically representing a woman's surname prior to marriage for identification and record linkage.

marital.marital: The patient's marital status, describing the individual's current relationship status for demographic and contact purposes.

race.race: The patient's racial classification, capturing demographic information for healthcare equity, research, and personalized care.

ethnicity.ethnicity: The patient's ethnic background, providing cultural and demographic context for culturally competent healthcare delivery.

gender.gender: The patient's gender identity, representing an important demographic characteristic for medical treatment and record-keeping.

birthplace.birthplace: The location where the patient was born, providing geographic origin information for medical and demographic records.

address.address: The patient's current street address, representing the primary residential location for contact and service delivery.

city.city: The city component of the patient's current address, specifying the municipal location for geographic reference.

state.state: The state or province component of the patient's current address, indicating the regional jurisdiction for the individual.

zip.zip: The postal or ZIP code for the patient's current address, enabling mail delivery and geographic analysis of patient populations.

note_id.note_id: A unique identifier for each clinical note or documentation entry, serving as the primary key for tracking individual medical notes.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient or person who is the subject of this clinical note, representing the individual whose medical information is documented.

note_event_id.note_event_id: An identifier linking to the specific clinical event or occurrence that prompted the creation of this note, connecting the documentation to its triggering event.

note_event_field_concept_id.note_event_field_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier classifying the type of event field associated with this note, providing categorization of the note's event context.

note_date.note_date: The calendar date when the clinical note was created or documented, indicating when the medical information was recorded.

note_datetime.note_datetime: The precise date and time when the clinical note was created, providing timestamp-level accuracy for when the documentation occurred.

note_type_concept_id.note_type_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier classifying the type or purpose of this clinical note, such as progress note, discharge summary, or consultation note.

note_class_concept_id.note_class_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the clinical class or category of this note, providing hierarchical classification of the documentation.

note_title.note_title: The title or heading of the clinical note, providing a brief descriptive summary of the note's content or purpose.

note_text.note_text: The actual content and textual information contained in the clinical note, comprising the detailed medical documentation and observations.

encoding_concept_id.encoding_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the text encoding format used in the note, specifying how the note text is digitally encoded.

language_concept_id.language_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the language in which the clinical note is written, indicating the linguistic format of the documentation.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider or clinician who created or authored this clinical note, representing the documenting professional.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the clinical visit or encounter during which this note was created, connecting the documentation to a specific healthcare visit.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier for more granular visit details or sub-elements within a visit occurrence, providing additional context about the note's clinical setting.

note_source_value.note_source_value: The original identifier or value for this note from the source clinical system, preserving the raw data before standardization.

date.date: The date when the medical procedure was performed during this encounter, providing temporal context for the healthcare event.

patient.patient: An identifier for the individual who received the medical procedure, linking this procedure record to the patient's overall healthcare history.

encounter.encounter: A unique identifier for the specific medical visit or encounter where this procedure was performed, serving as the primary key for tracking healthcare events.

code.code: A standardized medical code identifying the type of procedure performed, typically following healthcare coding systems like CPT or ICD.

description.description: A textual explanation of the medical procedure that was performed, providing human-readable details about the healthcare intervention.

cost.cost: The monetary expense associated with performing this medical procedure, representing the financial aspect of the healthcare service.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A medical code identifying the clinical indication or diagnosis that justified performing this procedure.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual explanation of the medical reason or diagnosis that necessitated performing this procedure.

provider_id.provider_id: A unique identifier for each healthcare provider, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual medical practitioners in the system.

provider_name.provider_name: The full name of the healthcare provider, representing the personal identifier of the medical professional.

npi.npi: The National Provider Identifier, a unique 10-digit identification number for covered healthcare providers in the United States.

dea.dea: The Drug Enforcement Administration registration number, authorizing the provider to prescribe controlled substances under federal regulations.

specialty_concept_id.specialty_concept_id: A foreign key reference to the standardized concept table that defines the provider's medical specialty or area of practice.

care_site_id.care_site_id: A foreign key linking to the care site or healthcare facility where the provider primarily practices or is affiliated.

year_of_birth.year_of_birth: The birth year of the healthcare provider, indicating their age or generation for demographic and analysis purposes.

gender_concept_id.gender_concept_id: A foreign key reference to the standardized concept table that defines the provider's gender identity.

provider_source_value.provider_source_value: The original identifier or code for the provider from the source data system, before standardization.

specialty_source_value.specialty_source_value: The original specialty code or description from the source system, representing the provider's area of practice before concept mapping.

specialty_source_concept_id.specialty_source_concept_id: A foreign key reference to the source concept table for medical specialties, capturing the original coding system's representation.

gender_source_value.gender_source_value: The original gender code or value from the source system, representing gender information before standardization.

gender_source_concept_id.gender_source_concept_id: A foreign key reference to the source concept table for gender, preserving the original coding system's representation of gender.

measurement_id.measurement_id: A unique identifier for each measurement record, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual measurement events in the healthcare system.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient or person who underwent the measurement, connecting this clinical observation to the individual's health record.

measurement_concept_id.measurement_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier defining what was measured (e.g., blood pressure, glucose level), categorizing the type of clinical observation.

measurement_date.measurement_date: The calendar date when the measurement was taken, providing the temporal context for when this clinical observation occurred.

measurement_datetime.measurement_datetime: The precise date and time when the measurement was recorded, offering detailed temporal context for the clinical observation event.

measurement_time.measurement_time: The time of day when the measurement was taken, complementing the date information for temporal precision.

measurement_type_concept_id.measurement_type_concept_id: A concept identifier classifying the measurement type or context (e.g., lab test, vital sign, survey response), describing how the measurement was obtained.

operator_concept_id.operator_concept_id: A concept identifier for mathematical operators (such as >, <, or =) that may qualify the measurement value when exact quantification isn't available.

value_as_number.value_as_number: The quantitative result of the measurement expressed as a numeric value, representing the actual measured quantity when applicable.

value_as_concept_id.value_as_concept_id: A concept identifier for categorical measurement results, used when the measurement outcome is qualitative rather than quantitative.

unit_concept_id.unit_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the measurement units (e.g., mg/dL, mmHg), defining the scale in which the numeric value is expressed.

range_low.range_low: The lower boundary of the normal reference range for this measurement, providing clinical context for result interpretation.

range_high.range_high: The upper boundary of the normal reference range for this measurement, establishing the upper limit for expected values.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who performed or ordered the measurement, documenting clinical responsibility.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare visit during which this measurement was taken, providing encounter context.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier for more granular visit information when applicable, offering detailed encounter context for the measurement.

measurement_source_value.measurement_source_value: The original code or value for the measurement as it appeared in the source data system, preserving raw data provenance.

measurement_source_concept_id.measurement_source_concept_id: A concept identifier mapping to the source system's coding scheme, facilitating data integration from multiple sources.

unit_source_value.unit_source_value: The original unit of measurement as recorded in the source data system, maintaining data provenance before standardization.

value_source_value.value_source_value: The original measurement value as recorded in the source data system, preserving raw data before any transformation or standardization.

procedure_occurrence_id.procedure_occurrence_id: A unique identifier for each medical procedure occurrence, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual procedure events in the healthcare system.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient who underwent the procedure, representing the individual recipient of this medical intervention.

procedure_concept_id.procedure_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the medical procedure performed, linking to a controlled vocabulary that defines the specific procedure type.

procedure_date.procedure_date: The calendar date when the medical procedure was performed, indicating when this healthcare intervention occurred.

procedure_datetime.procedure_datetime: The precise date and time when the medical procedure was performed, providing detailed temporal context for the procedure occurrence.

procedure_type_concept_id.procedure_type_concept_id: A classification identifier for the type of procedure (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, emergency), categorizing the context of this medical intervention.

modifier_concept_id.modifier_concept_id: An identifier for additional procedure modifiers that provide supplementary details about how or where the procedure was performed.

quantity.quantity: The number of times the procedure was performed or the count of procedure units, measuring the volume or frequency of this medical intervention.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who performed or oversaw the procedure, representing the medical professional responsible for this intervention.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare visit during which the procedure occurred, connecting this procedure to its clinical encounter context.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier for more granular visit details, providing additional context about the specific setting or sub-encounter where the procedure took place.

procedure_source_value.procedure_source_value: The original procedure code or description from the source data system, representing the raw, unstandardized procedure information.

procedure_source_concept_id.procedure_source_concept_id: An identifier linking to the source concept system, connecting the original procedure code to its source vocabulary.

modifier_source_value.modifier_source_value: The original modifier code or description from the source data system, representing the raw, unstandardized modifier information for the procedure.

observation_id.observation_id: A unique identifier for each observation record, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual clinical observations.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient or person who is the subject of this observation, representing who was observed.

observation_concept_id.observation_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier defining what was observed, such as a specific clinical measurement or finding.

observation_date.observation_date: The calendar date when the observation was made, providing the temporal context for when this clinical data was recorded.

observation_datetime.observation_datetime: The precise date and time when the observation occurred, offering detailed temporal context for the clinical event.

observation_type_concept_id.observation_type_concept_id: A concept identifier classifying the type of observation, such as laboratory test, physical exam finding, or patient-reported outcome.

value_as_number.value_as_number: The numerical result of the observation when the measurement is quantitative, representing the observed value in numeric form.

value_as_string.value_as_string: The textual result of the observation when the measurement is qualitative, capturing the observation result as text.

value_as_concept_id.value_as_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for coded observation results, used when the value is categorical rather than numeric.

qualifier_concept_id.qualifier_concept_id: A concept identifier for qualifiers that modify the observation, such as anatomical location or severity descriptors.

unit_concept_id.unit_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the measurement unit associated with numeric observation values.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who performed or recorded this observation.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the clinical visit during which this observation was made, providing encounter context.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier linking to more detailed visit information, offering granular context about the observation setting.

observation_source_value.observation_source_value: The original code or text from the source system that describes what was observed before standardization.

observation_source_concept_id.observation_source_concept_id: A concept identifier mapping the source observation value to the standardized concept system.

unit_source_value.unit_source_value: The original unit of measurement from the source system before standardization to concept identifiers.

qualifier_source_value.qualifier_source_value: The original qualifier text or code from the source system before standardization.

observation_event_id.observation_event_id: An identifier linking this observation to a related observation event or grouping in the data.

obs_event_field_concept_id.obs_event_field_concept_id: A concept identifier defining the specific field or aspect within an observation event that this record represents.

value_as_datetime.value_as_datetime: A date and time value for observations where the result itself is temporal, such as time of symptom onset.

person_id.person_id: A unique identifier for an individual person, serving as the primary key for this table and the main subject of the record.

gender_concept_id.gender_concept_id: A standardized code representing the person's gender, linked to a concept reference table as a foreign key.

year_of_birth.year_of_birth: The year component of the person's birth date, a temporal attribute of the individual.

month_of_birth.month_of_birth: The month component of the person's birth date, a temporal attribute of the individual.

day_of_birth.day_of_birth: The day component of the person's birth date, a temporal attribute of the individual.

birth_datetime.birth_datetime: The precise date and time of the person's birth, providing a complete temporal attribute for the individual.

death_datetime.death_datetime: The precise date and time of the person's death, if recorded, indicating the end of life for the individual.

race_concept_id.race_concept_id: A standardized code representing the person's race, linked to a concept reference table as a foreign key.

ethnicity_concept_id.ethnicity_concept_id: A standardized code representing the person's ethnicity, linked to a concept reference table as a foreign key.

location_id.location_id: An identifier linking the person to a geographic location or address, serving as a foreign key to a location table.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking the person to their primary healthcare provider, serving as a foreign key to a provider table.

care_site_id.care_site_id: An identifier linking the person to a healthcare facility or organization, serving as a foreign key to a care site table.

person_source_value.person_source_value: The original identifier or code for the person from the source data system, before standardization.

gender_source_value.gender_source_value: The original text value for the person's gender as recorded in the source data system.

gender_source_concept_id.gender_source_concept_id: A code mapping the source gender value to a standardized concept, acting as a foreign key to a concept table.

race_source_value.race_source_value: The original text value for the person's race as recorded in the source data system.

race_source_concept_id.race_source_concept_id: A code mapping the source race value to a standardized concept, acting as a foreign key to a concept table.

ethnicity_source_value.ethnicity_source_value: The original text value for the person's ethnicity as recorded in the source data system.

ethnicity_source_concept_id.ethnicity_source_concept_id: A code mapping the source ethnicity value to a standardized concept, acting as a foreign key to a concept table.

specimen_id.specimen_id: A unique identifier for each biological specimen, serving as the primary key for tracking individual medical samples in the system.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient from whom the specimen was collected, representing the individual source of this biological sample.

specimen_concept_id.specimen_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the type of specimen collected, categorizing the biological material type of this sample.

specimen_type_concept_id.specimen_type_concept_id: A concept identifier for how the specimen was obtained or its collection type, classifying the methodology or context of specimen acquisition.

specimen_date.specimen_date: The calendar date when the specimen was collected from the patient, indicating when this biological sample was obtained.

specimen_datetime.specimen_datetime: The precise date and time when the specimen was collected, providing exact temporal context for when this sample was acquired.

quantity.quantity: The measured amount or volume of the specimen collected, representing the physical quantity of this biological sample.

unit_concept_id.unit_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier for the unit of measurement of the specimen quantity, defining the measurement scale for this sample's amount.

anatomic_site_concept_id.anatomic_site_concept_id: A concept identifier for the anatomical location on the body where the specimen was collected, specifying the physical origin site of this sample.

disease_status_concept_id.disease_status_concept_id: A concept identifier for the patient's disease status at the time of specimen collection, indicating health context when this sample was obtained.

specimen_source_id.specimen_source_id: The original identifier for the specimen from the source data system, representing the external reference for this biological sample.

specimen_source_value.specimen_source_value: The original text description of the specimen type from the source system, providing the raw classification of this biological material.

unit_source_value.unit_source_value: The original text representation of the measurement unit from the source system, showing how the specimen quantity was originally recorded.

anatomic_site_source_value.anatomic_site_source_value: The original text description of the anatomical collection site from the source system, providing the raw location data for where this sample was taken.

disease_status_source_value.disease_status_source_value: The original text description of the disease status from the source system, showing how the patient's health condition was originally recorded.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A unique identifier for each healthcare visit record, serving as the primary key for this table.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the person or patient who had the visit, representing the subject of care.

visit_concept_id.visit_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier defining the type of visit (e.g., inpatient, outpatient).

visit_start_date.visit_start_date: The calendar date when the visit began, indicating the start day of the healthcare encounter.

visit_start_datetime.visit_start_datetime: The precise date and time when the visit began, providing a detailed start timestamp.

visit_end_date.visit_end_date: The calendar date when the visit ended, indicating the conclusion day of the healthcare encounter.

visit_end_datetime.visit_end_datetime: The precise date and time when the visit ended, providing a detailed end timestamp.

visit_type_concept_id.visit_type_concept_id: A concept identifier indicating the provenance or method of how the visit record was created (e.g., EHR, claim).

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider responsible for or associated with the visit.

care_site_id.care_site_id: An identifier linking to the care site or facility where the visit took place.

visit_source_value.visit_source_value: The original source code or identifier for the visit as it appeared in the raw data source.

visit_source_concept_id.visit_source_concept_id: A concept identifier that maps to the original visit source value for standardization.

admitted_from_concept_id.admitted_from_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier representing the origin or source of admission for the visit.

admitted_from_source_value.admitted_from_source_value: The original source code or text describing where the patient was admitted from.

discharge_to_concept_id.discharge_to_concept_id: A standardized concept identifier representing the destination or disposition after the visit.

discharge_to_source_value.discharge_to_source_value: The original source code or text describing where the patient was discharged to.

preceding_visit_occurrence_id.preceding_visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to a previous visit record, establishing a sequence of visits for the same person.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A unique identifier for each visit detail record, serving as the primary key that distinguishes individual healthcare visit components.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient or person receiving healthcare services, representing the individual associated with this visit detail.

visit_detail_concept_id.visit_detail_concept_id: A standardized code classifying the type of visit detail, categorizing the specific healthcare service or encounter component.

visit_detail_start_date.visit_detail_start_date: The calendar date when this visit detail began, indicating the starting day of the healthcare service component.

visit_detail_start_datetime.visit_detail_start_datetime: The precise date and time when this visit detail began, providing exact timing for the start of the healthcare service.

visit_detail_end_date.visit_detail_end_date: The calendar date when this visit detail ended, indicating the concluding day of the healthcare service component.

visit_detail_end_datetime.visit_detail_end_datetime: The precise date and time when this visit detail ended, providing exact timing for the conclusion of the healthcare service.

visit_detail_type_concept_id.visit_detail_type_concept_id: A code indicating how this visit detail was recorded or originated, classifying the data capture method or source type.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider responsible for this visit detail, representing the clinician or caregiver involved.

care_site_id.care_site_id: An identifier linking to the healthcare facility or location where this visit detail occurred, specifying the physical site of service.

visit_detail_source_value.visit_detail_source_value: The original value for the visit detail as it appeared in the source data, preserving the raw information before standardization.

visit_detail_source_concept_id.visit_detail_source_concept_id: A concept identifier from the source system for this visit detail, linking to the original coding scheme used.

admitted_from_source_value.admitted_from_source_value: The original source value indicating where the patient was admitted from prior to this visit detail, showing the preceding location.

admitted_from_concept_id.admitted_from_concept_id: A standardized code classifying where the patient was admitted from prior to this visit detail, categorizing the origin location.

discharge_to_source_value.discharge_to_source_value: The original source value indicating where the patient was discharged to after this visit detail, showing the subsequent destination.

discharge_to_concept_id.discharge_to_concept_id: A standardized code classifying where the patient was discharged to after this visit detail, categorizing the destination location.

preceding_visit_detail_id.preceding_visit_detail_id: An identifier linking to the previous visit detail in a temporal sequence, establishing chronological order between visit components.

visit_detail_parent_id.visit_detail_parent_id: An identifier linking to a parent visit detail record, representing a hierarchical relationship between nested visit components.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the broader visit occurrence that contains this detail, connecting the component to its overall healthcare visit context.
